summary: Implement authoritative save, load, restart, and crash-recovery flows for Web and Electron
acceptance_criteria: Save requests obtain an authoritative Worker snapshot and emit the versioned SaveEnvelope; local UI state or the M5/M6 session JSON shape cannot be presented as a game save.
Web and Electron persist and load the same logical save format and deterministic bytes for identical metadata and world state, with platform differences isolated behind PlatformAdapter.
Electron file access uses context-isolated, allowlisted preload IPC with validated payloads and no Node integration, arbitrary path access, raw filesystem bridge, or renderer-owned authority.

Load validates size, schema, version, checksum, content compatibility, and migration boundaries before replacing the Worker session; failure preserves the current session and reports an explicit reason.
Save, terminate all runtime state, restart the app/Worker, and load reproduces the same authoritative state hash, calendar, scheduler tail, and read-model revision baseline.

Unexpected Worker termination offers bounded recovery from the latest committed recovery save or an explicit non-recoverable state; it never silently boots a fixture or reports a successful no-op recovery.
Corrupt, truncated, oversized, incompatible, and tampered saves are rejected without deleting or overwriting the source save.
required_tests: golden and negative tests cover deterministic bytes, Web/Electron parity, restart hash, scheduler tail, corrupt/truncated/oversized/incompatible saves, and Worker crash recovery
notes: This task requires independent security review in addition to systems and QA review.
Shared Worker and protocol contracts remain owned by RR1-WEB-WORKER-RUNTIME-001.
